papers.title: Reframing Through New Minds: How External Experts Unlock Problem Reframing Through Reasoning Logics
papers.abstract: This study examines how external experts assist organizations in reframing problems by applying different reasoning logics during early innovation stages. Research emphasizes the need to interpret environmental ambiguity and reframe problems. External experts contribute by offering unconventional perspectives that help shift how problems are understood, drawing on five reasoning logics: reverse deduction, reverse induction, explanatory abduction, speculative abduction, and moral abduction.
papers.summary_ja: 外部専門家が5種類の推論ロジック（逆演繹・逆帰納・説明的アブダクション・思索的アブダクション・道徳的アブダクション）を用いることで、組織が問題をリフレーミングし革新的な洞察を得るプロセスを明らかにしたエスノグラフィック研究。

papers.title: Co-Design at the Boundary: Understanding the Dynamics of Open Innovation Between Companies and Communities
papers.abstract: Over two decades, firms have built platforms and engaged open innovation communities to improve and customize products through widened participation in the design process. While benefits of involving external co-designers have been documented, how co-design processes unfold over time at organizational boundaries remains poorly understood. This five-year ethnographic study identifies three co-design types (intimate, instrumental, limited) and reveals how driving forces prompt dynamic transitions between them.
papers.summary_ja: 自動車メーカーとオンラインコミュニティの5年間のコデザイン協働を追跡し、「親密型・手段型・限定型」の3形態が環境・組織要因により動態的に移行することを明らかにした、境界研究の最前線。

papers.title: Enhancing collaboration and knowledge sharing through intra-organizational platforms: a design science research study
papers.abstract: Platforms have revolutionized value creation by enabling efficient matchmaking between customer groups. While traditionally associated with external value exchange, platform thinking represents the mechanism that, through matchmaking, supports established firms to innovate by orchestrating available resources. While platforms are known in the literature for creating value by efficiently connecting external customer groups, their potential to support efficient collaboration among internal units is largely underexplored. This design science research study develops and evaluates an intra-organizational platform to enhance collaboration and knowledge sharing within established firms, identifying six design principles for effective internal platform deployment.
papers.summary_ja: 「プラットフォーム思考」を組織内部に適用し、既存企業が社内ユニット間の知識共有と協働を加速できることをデザインサイエンス研究アプローチで実証した、イントラ・プラットフォーム研究の先駆的論文。

papers.title: Artificial intelligence, dynamic capabilities, and innovation resilience: The contingent roles of market competition intensity and technological turbulence
papers.abstract: Study examines how artificial intelligence improves innovation resilience via dynamic capabilities, using panel data from 3,958 Chinese firms (2011-2023) with fixed-effect models. Absorptive capability mediates approximately 50% of the AI-to-resilience effect. Market competition intensity and technological turbulence negatively moderate the AI-dynamic capabilities relationship, suggesting environmental conditions critically shape AI's innovation benefits.
papers.summary_ja: 中国企業3,958社の2011〜2023年パネルデータを用い、AIが吸収能力を主経路としてダイナミック・ケイパビリティを強化しイノベーション・レジリエンスを高めること、ただし競争激化・技術激変環境ではその効果が減弱することを大規模実証。

papers.title: Leveraging intellectual capital and dynamic capabilities for service innovation: insights from management consulting firms
papers.abstract: This study examines how intellectual capital components - human, structural, relational and renewal capital - impact service innovation performance in Chinese management consulting firms. It also investigates the mediating role of dynamic capabilities in transforming intellectual capital into innovative service outcomes, providing insights into how consulting firms can strategically leverage their intangible resources and organizational processes to enhance service innovation performance.
papers.summary_ja: 中国コンサルティング企業を対象に、人的・構造的・関係的・更新資本の4種の知的資本がダイナミック・ケイパビリティを介してサービスイノベーション成果に影響することを実証した、B2Bサービス企業に直結する研究。

papers.title: Balancing speed-novelty innovation paradox in new ventures: The role of design thinking and ambidextrous learning
papers.journal: Journal of Engineering and Technology Management
papers.abstract: This study examines how new ventures can balance the inherent tension between speed and novelty in innovation - the speed-novelty paradox. Drawing on design thinking and ambidextrous learning theory, the research investigates mechanisms by which ventures can simultaneously pursue rapid execution and novel ideation without sacrificing either imperative.
papers.summary_ja: 新規事業における「スピードと新規性のトレードオフ（イノベーションパラドックス）」を克服するうえで、デザイン思考と両利き学習（探索・活用の統合）が鍵となることを実証した研究。

papers.title: Cumulative processes in multi-system transitions: expanding value chains and limits to growth for sustainable alternatives
papers.abstract: Longitudinal analysis of the evolution of biomethane in Sweden. Linkage systems connect upstream and downstream systems in new value chains. Niche accumulation in downstream systems comes with cumulative upstream processes. Different systems follow distinct yet complementary transition pathways. This paper examines the long-term evolution of biomethane in Sweden as a case of a multi-system transition, identifying how linkage systems bridge upstream and downstream actors while creating cumulative processes that expand value chains and simultaneously impose limits to growth for sustainable alternatives.
papers.summary_ja: スウェーデンのバイオメタン産業の長期縦断分析を通じ、複数セクターを横断する「リンケージシステム」が持続可能技術の価値連鎖拡大と成長限界の両方をもたらす累積的プロセスを解明した移行研究。

papers.title: Digital transformation through innovation: The human-AI decision spectrum
papers.authors: Yufeng Zhang, Xiaojun Wang, Shlomo Y. Tarba, Andy Neely
papers.abstract: This study conceptualizes how humans and AI share decision-making responsibilities in digital transformation contexts, presenting a spectrum model for understanding the division of cognitive labor between human and AI agents in innovation processes. The framework provides a typology of human-AI decision arrangements from fully human-directed to fully AI-directed, with implications for organizational design and innovation management.
papers.summary_ja: デジタルトランスフォーメーションにおいて人間とAIがどのように意思決定を分担・補完し合うかを「スペクトラム」として概念化し、イノベーション管理への含意を論じた論文。著者陣にはTarba（戦略的提携）・Neely（パフォーマンス測定）という世界的権威が名を連ねる。

papers.title: Artificial intelligence driven dynamic capabilities: unpacking micro-foundational mechanisms
papers.abstract: As AI becomes integrated into organizational processes, SMEs face unique challenges and opportunities in developing dynamic capabilities for digital transformation. Study draws on 51 interviews from 28 Finnish SMEs to unpack micro-foundational mechanisms of AI-driven dynamic capabilities, identifying strategic leadership framing, informal team learning, and human-AI trust norms as key components.
papers.summary_ja: フィンランドSME28社51名へのインタビューを通じ、戦略的リーダーシップの枠組み・非公式チーム学習・人間-AI信頼規範の3要素がダイナミック・ケイパビリティの微視的基盤を形成することを質的に明らかにした研究。

papers.title: Temporal Viability Theory: A Cybernetic Framework for Time-Bound Viability in Socio-Technical and Algorithmic Systems
papers.abstract: This article reconceptualises viability in organisational cybernetics as a temporal phenomenon, arguing that the persistence of socio-technical systems cannot be understood apart from their temporal structure. Drawing on Stafford Beer's Viable System Model (VSM), a temporal-viability operator is proposed that defines viability as a finite, recursively accumulated timespan generated by the interplay of systemic growth, decay and intervention.
papers.summary_ja: ヴァイアブル・システム・モデル（VSM）を時間軸で再概念化し、社会技術系・アルゴリズム系の持続可能性は「時間的生存力」として測定・管理できるという新しいサイバネティクス理論を提示した論文。
